seoTitle: Lords Hill vs Lorne — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Lords Hill and Lorne. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Lords Hill vs Lorne: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lords Hill and Lorne.
heading: Lords Hill vs Lorne — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lords Hill, New South Wales and Lorne, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lords Hill has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Lorne (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lords Hill or Lorne safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lords Hill has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Lorne scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lords Hill scores 24/100 for bushfire risk. Lorne scores 33/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lords Hill or Lorne?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lords Hill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
